text: ESCALATION 1: If the customer explicitly threatens legal action, consumer court, or lawsuit, immediately stop troubleshooting. Say: 'I understand you are taking this seriously. Let me connect you to our Grievance Redressal Manager.' Route to Escalation Matrix Level 2.

ESCALATION 2: If the customer mentions contacting TRAI, the FCC, or the Telecom Ombudsman regarding an unresolved issue, acknowledge their right to do so and immediately escalate the call to the Regulatory Desk or Floor Manager.

ESCALATION 3: If this is the 4th time or more the customer is calling about the exact same technical issue within 48 hours, apologize profusely and immediately transfer to a Tier 2 Technical Supervisor. Do not make them troubleshoot again.

ESCALATION 4: If a customer claims massive financial loss or business disruption due to network outage (Enterprise/Business impact), validate their frustration and transfer the call to the B2B Enterprise Support Manager.

ESCALATION 5: If the customer claims severe identity theft, unauthorized SIM swap, or money stolen from their bank via our network, immediately lock the account and transfer to the Fraud & Security Manager.

ESCALATION 6: If the customer threatens physical harm to themselves or others, or sounds in severe medical distress without signal to call an ambulance, keep them on the line, speak calmly, and silently flag the emergency manager to contact local authorities.

ESCALATION 7: If the customer is an identified VIP, politician, or media personality threatening social media backlash, apologize for the inconvenience and route directly to the Priority PR Support Manager.

ESCALATION 8: If the customer demands to speak to the CEO, Director, or 'Highest Authority', politely say: 'I understand your frustration and I want to ensure this gets the highest attention. Let me connect you with our Floor Manager who handles executive escalations.'
ESCALATION 9: If a physical altercation or extreme abuse is reported at a retail physical store, log code RED-99, ensure the customer is safe, and escalate to the Regional Retail Manager.

ESCALATION 10: If there is an unexplained, systemic billing error affecting the customer repeatedly over 3 months despite previous tickets, do not offer another temporary credit. Escalate to the Billing Operations Manager for a permanent account audit.

APOLOGY 1: Systemic Outage. If there is a known regional outage, say: 'I sincerely apologize for the disruption. Our engineers are already working on a fiber cut in your area. Service will be restored by [Time]. Thank you for your patience.'

APOLOGY 2: Missed Technician Appointment. If a broadband tech didn't show up, say: 'I am so sorry the technician missed the window. Your time is valuable. I am prioritizing your ticket for immediate dispatch and applying a ₹100 inconvenience credit.'
APOLOGY 3: Billing Error Admitted. If the company made a mistake, own it. Say: 'You are absolutely right, and I apologize for this oversight on our end. I am reversing the charge immediately.'

APOLOGY 4: Long Wait Time. If the CRM shows they waited over 5 minutes on hold, begin with: 'I am so sorry for the long wait time today, thank you for holding. How can I resolve your issue immediately?'
APOLOGY 5: Agent Dropped Call. If following up after a disconnected call, say: 'I apologize that our previous call disconnected. I have your details pulled up so you don't have to repeat yourself.'

THANKING 1: 5+ Year Loyalty. When opening the call for a long-term customer, say: 'First, I want to thank you for being a loyal customer with us for over [X] years. We truly value your relationship. How can I assist you?'

THANKING 2: Troubleshooting Patience. After a long technical troubleshooting step, say: 'Thank you so much for your patience while we tried those steps. I know it takes time, but it really helps me narrow down the issue.'
THANKING 3: Prompt Payment. If a customer calls after paying a late bill, say: 'Thank you for clearing your outstanding dues. Your services have been fully restored.'
THANKING 4: Constructive Feedback. If a customer reports an app bug or UI issue, say: 'Thank you for bringing this to our attention. Feedback like yours helps us improve our app for everyone.'
THANKING 5: Plan Upgrade. If a customer upgrades their plan during the call, say: 'Thank you for choosing our premium Xstream plan. You are going to love the extra speed and OTT benefits.'

DE-ESCALATION 1: Customer Shouting. Do not raise your voice. Wait for a 2-second pause, then speak softly and slowly: 'I hear how frustrated you are, and I am committed to fixing this for you right now. Let's look at the account together.'

DE-ESCALATION 2: Interrupting Customer. If the customer keeps interrupting, stop talking immediately. Let them finish venting. Then say: 'I understand completely. Please allow me to check the system so I can give you the exact solution.'

DE-ESCALATION 3: Personal Insults. If the customer insults the agent personally, remain objective: 'Sir/Ma'am, I want to help you resolve this, but I must ask that we keep the conversation professional. How can I assist with your connection?'

DE-ESCALATION 4: Refusal to Troubleshoot. If the angry customer says 'I already did that, fix it from your end', say: 'I completely understand you've tried steps already. To help me push this to the engineering team, I just need to verify one setting with you.'

DE-ESCALATION 5: 'You people are thieves'. If accused of stealing, do not get defensive. Say: 'I assure you we value your money. Let's go through the bill line-by-line together so I can show you exactly where this charge came from and see if it can be removed.'

EDGE CASE: Deceased Account Holder. If a family member calls to cancel a deceased person's line, express condolences: 'I am so sorry for your loss.' Do not ask for standard OTP. Advise them to email the death certificate to the nodal desk to close the account with zero penalty.

EDGE CASE: Minor Calling. If the caller sounds like a young child requesting plan changes, inform them politely that an adult account holder must verify the changes. Do not make billing changes for minors.

EDGE CASE: Police Inquiry. If a caller claims to be a police officer asking for CDR (Call Detail Records) or location tracking, politely state: 'Officer, all Law Enforcement inquiries must be routed through the Nodal Officer portal with an official warrant.' Do not provide data.

EDGE CASE: Phishing Report. If a customer says someone called asking for OTP claiming to be from Airtel/Jio, warn them: 'Please do not share the OTP. We will never call to ask for OTPs. I am flagging your account for high security.'

EDGE CASE: Harassment Calls. If a customer is receiving threatening calls from another number, explain that we cannot block specific numbers at the network level, but guide them to use the smartphone's built-in block feature and advise contacting the police.

NETWORK: If a customer complains about slow 5G speeds, ask them to check if they have crossed their daily FUP limit of 1.5GB/2GB. If FUP is crossed, speeds throttle to 64kbps. Suggest buying a data voucher.

NETWORK: If a customer has no signal indoors, explain that thick concrete walls block high-frequency bands. Ask them to enable 'VoWiFi' or 'Wi-Fi Calling' in their phone settings. Do not offer network credits for indoor coverage.
NETWORK: Call Drops. If calls are disconnecting frequently while traveling, explain it may be a cell-tower handoff issue. Ask them to restart their phone to refresh the network latch.
NETWORK: SMS Not Sending. If a customer cannot send SMS, ask them to check if they have an active plan. Unlimited plans without SMS quota or expired validity will block outgoing SMS.
NETWORK: No Internet (Data ON). If the data toggle is ON but internet isn't working, ask them to check their APN (Access Point Name) settings. Reset APN to default.

NETWORK: 5G Not Showing. If a customer has a 5G phone but only gets 4G, ensure they are in a 5G coverage area and have updated their phone's OS to the latest version. SIM change is not required for 5G.
NETWORK: Cross-State Roaming. If the customer lost network after crossing state lines, tell them to turn on 'Data Roaming' in their mobile settings. Domestic roaming is free.
NETWORK: Complete No Service. Ask the customer to toggle Airplane Mode on and off. If that fails, ask them to remove the SIM, wipe the gold contacts with a clean cloth, and reinsert.

NETWORK: International Roaming No Signal. Tell the customer to go to settings and change 'Network Selection' from Automatic to Manual, and select a partner network (e.g., AT&T in USA, Vodafone in UK).
NETWORK: VoLTE Missing. If the customer cannot make calls and browse data simultaneously, ensure VoLTE (Voice over LTE) is enabled in their cellular settings.

BROADBAND: Router LOS Red Light. Blinking red LOS means the optical fiber is cut or bent. Ask the customer to check the white cable. Schedule a technician. Inform about a ₹150 charge if cut inside the house.
BROADBAND: PON Light Blinking. This indicates backend authentication failure. The agent must send a remote reset command to the router from the CRM. Ask the customer to wait 5 minutes.
BROADBAND: Slow Wi-Fi Speeds. Inform the customer to connect to the 5GHz network for speed, rather than 2.4GHz, and to stand in the same room as the router to test.
BROADBAND: Ping/Latency Spikes. For gamers complaining about lag, advise them to use a direct LAN (Ethernet) cable instead of Wi-Fi for stable latency.
BROADBAND: Forgot Wi-Fi Password. Guide the customer to open the official company app, go to 'My Wi-Fi', and they can view or change their password directly from the app.
BROADBAND: Router Overheating. If the router is extremely hot and dropping connections, advise the customer to keep it in an open area, not inside a closed TV cabinet.
BROADBAND: Mesh Node Disconnected. If the secondary mesh unit is red, ask the customer to move it closer to the primary router to establish a strong backhaul connection.
BROADBAND: No Internet on One Device. If all phones work but one laptop doesn't, inform the customer the issue is with the laptop's network adapter or IP settings, not the broadband line.
BROADBAND: Relocation Request. If the customer is moving houses, inform them to raise a 'Shift' request via the app. A new wire will be laid at the new address for a ₹300 shifting fee.
BROADBAND: Fiber Cut Compensation. If a major underground fiber cut causes an outage for more than 24 hours, automatically offer a pro-rata credit for 2 days to the next bill cycle.
BROADBAND: Default Router IP. If a tech-savvy user wants to log into the router admin page, provide the default IP: 192.168.1.1 and default login credentials.
BROADBAND: MAC Binding Error. If a customer changed their own router and internet stopped, explain that the MAC address is bound. Agent must release the MAC binding in the CRM.
BROADBAND: Intermittent Drops. Ask the customer to unplug the router from the power socket, wait 30 seconds, and plug it back in to clear the cache.
BROADBAND: Static IP Request. Inform the customer that Static IPs are only available for commercial/enterprise connections or specific high-tier residential plans at an extra ₹200/month.
BROADBAND: Installation Pending. If a new customer calls asking about installation status, check the dispatch system. Standard SLA is 48 hours from payment.
BILLING: VAS Deduction. If money was deducted automatically for caller tunes or games, explain how to deactivate by sending SMS 'STOP' to 155223. Offer one-time courtesy refund if angry.

BILLING: International Roaming Shock. Check if an IR pack was active. If not, standard PAYG rates apply. If extremely angry and threatening to port, offer retroactive IR pack with supervisor AUTH-IR code.
BILLING: Late Fee Waiver. If a customer is charged a ₹100 late fee and has a good 6-month payment history, agent is authorized to waive the late fee as a one-time gesture of goodwill.
BILLING: Double Payment. If the customer paid twice due to a bank glitch, assure them the extra amount will be automatically adjusted as an advance credit against the next month's bill.
BILLING: Refund Timeline. If a refund is initiated, inform the customer that RBI guidelines mandate 5 to 7 business days for the amount to reflect in their original payment method.

BILLING: Pro-Rata Charges. If a customer changes their postpaid plan mid-cycle, explain that the bill contains 'Pro-Rata' charges—meaning they are billed partially for the old plan and partially for the new plan based on the exact days used.
BILLING: Unbilled Amount. If a customer is confused about 'Unbilled Amount' on the app, explain that this is their current live usage that has not yet been generated into an official invoice.
BILLING: Payment Failed but Money Cut. Advise the customer to wait 24 hours. The bank will either settle the payment with us or auto-refund it to their account. Do not try paying again immediately.
BILLING: Auto-Pay Setup. Guide the customer to set up e-Mandate/Auto-pay via UPI or Credit Card on the app to avoid future late fees and get a ₹50 cashback on the first setup.

BILLING: Security Deposit Refund. If closing a postpaid account, inform the customer the initial security deposit will be refunded to their bank account 30 days after the final zero-due bill is generated.
BILLING: Paper Bill Request. If a customer wants a physical bill mailed to their home, inform them we are a green company. Paper bills incur a ₹50 monthly printing and courier charge.

BILLING: Credit Limit Reached. If outgoing services are barred mid-month, explain their usage exceeded their assigned credit limit. Ask them to make an interim 'Advance Payment' to instantly unbar services.
BILLING: GST Change. If the bill amount is slightly higher, remind the customer that telecom services attract 18% GST. The base plan price remains the same.
BILLING: Data Booster. If a postpaid user runs out of data, inform them they can add a ₹100/10GB data booster via the app, which will be added to their next billing cycle.
BILLING: Cheque Bounce. If a payment cheque bounces, inform the customer there is a ₹250 bank penalty charge applied to their account. Advise digital payments moving forward.
BILLING: Hidden Charges. Address claims of hidden charges by offering to email an itemized bill showing every call, SMS, and data session for complete transparency.
BILLING: Plan Validity Extension. Prepaid validity cannot be 'paused'. If they are traveling abroad, advise them to recharge with a cheap validity-extension pack to keep the number active.

BILLING: Wrong Number Recharge. If a customer recharged the wrong number by mistake, explain that telecom policy strictly prohibits reversing successful prepaid recharges. They must contact the person they recharged.
BILLING: Corporate Discount. If the customer's company offers a corporate tie-up, ask them to upload their employee ID on the portal to get a 10% discount on postpaid plans.
BILLING: Downgrade Penalty. Remind customers that downgrading to a lower plan within the first 3 months of activation may forfeit their waived installation or activation fees.
ACCOUNT: Porting Out (MNP). Ask the reason. If network, log priority ticket. If price, explain OTT bundle benefits. If they insist, tell them to SMS 'PORT <Number>' to 1900 to generate UPC.
ACCOUNT: Lost SIM/Phone. Immediately verify identity (DOB, Address) and block the SIM to prevent misuse. Visit store with Aadhaar Card for ₹50 replacement SIM. Activates in 2-4 hours.
ACCOUNT: PUK Code Blocked. If the customer entered the wrong SIM PIN 3 times, the SIM asks for PUK. Verify identity and provide the 8-digit PUK code from the CRM.
ACCOUNT: Convert Physical to eSIM. Tell the customer to ensure their phone supports eSIM. Send SMS 'eSIM <registered email>' to 121. Follow the QR code instructions sent to the email.
ACCOUNT: Pre to Postpaid Migration. Explain it can be done instantly via the app or store. Existing prepaid balance will be credited against the first postpaid bill.
ACCOUNT: Post to Prepaid Migration. Customer must clear all outstanding unbilled amounts first, then visit a retail store for KYC verification. Takes 48 hours to process.
ACCOUNT: Change Registered Mobile Number (RMN). RMN for broadband cannot be changed over phone for security. Customer must verify via Aadhaar OTP on the official app.
ACCOUNT: Ownership Transfer. To transfer a number to another person, both the current owner and new owner must visit a store with their respective KYC documents.
ACCOUNT: Family Plan Linking. Guide the customer to the 'Family Manager' section in the app to invite up to 4 family members to share their premium data pool.
ACCOUNT: Safe Custody. If a customer is traveling abroad for 6 months, offer 'Safe Custody' for ₹50/month to keep the number active without paying the full postpaid rental.
ACCOUNT: Fancy Number Request. Inform the customer that VIP/Fancy numbers can only be purchased at physical gallery stores via auction or premium upfront payment.
ACCOUNT: Address Change. For billing address updates, ask the customer to upload a new proof of address (e.g., Electricity Bill, Aadhaar) on the self-care portal.
ACCOUNT: DND Activation. If customer complains of spam calls, advise them to open the app or send SMS 'START 0' to 1909 to activate Full Do Not Disturb (DND). Takes 7 days to apply.

ACCOUNT: Reactivate Suspended Number. If a prepaid number is suspended due to 90 days of no recharge, inform them they have a 15-day grace period to recharge before the number is permanently recycled.
ACCOUNT: International Calling Activation (ISD). ISD is deactivated by default to prevent fraud. Verify customer identity and enable ISD services manually in the CRM.
COMPLIANCE: Age Restriction. We cannot issue a SIM card in the name of a minor (under 18). A parent or guardian must use their Aadhaar card for KYC.
COMPLIANCE: OTP Warning. Constantly remind customers never to share OTPs. 'Sir, for your security, neither I nor any company employee will ever ask for your OTP.'
COMPLIANCE: KYC Failure. If e-KYC is rejected, inform the customer their fingerprint did not match Aadhaar records. They must visit an Aadhaar Seva Kendra to update biometrics.
COMPLIANCE: Telemarketing Block. We cannot block specific telemarketers manually. Advise the customer to report spam numbers by sending SMS 'COMPLAINT <number>' to 1909.
COMPLIANCE: Call Recording Notice. If a customer asks, confirm that 'Yes, this call is being recorded for quality and training purposes.'
DTH: E16 Error Code. E16 means the DTH account is deactivated due to low balance. Tell the customer to recharge their account to restore TV services.
DTH: E48 Error Code. E48 means No Signal. Ask the customer if it is raining heavily (Rain Fade). If clear skies, ask them to check if the coaxial cable is tightly connected to the set-top box.
DTH: Channel Not Subscribed. If a customer wants to watch a specific match, guide them to the app to add the specific sports channel a-la-carte for ₹19/month.
DTH: Remote Not Working. Ask the customer to replace the batteries. If it still fails, advise them they can purchase a replacement remote at the nearest store for ₹150.
DTH: Heavy Rain Warning. Empathize: 'I apologize, DTH signals are affected by thick clouds and heavy rain. Services will automatically restore once the weather clears.'
